edges.description: HPV-associated adenocarcinomas are stratified by the Silva A/B/C pattern of stromal invasion, which directly predicts nodal metastasis risk.
edges.source: HPV E6/E7-Mediated Oncogenesis in Glandular Epithelium

edges.description: Invasive HPV-driven endocervical adenocarcinoma typically presents with abnormal vaginal bleeding.
edges.source: HPV E6/E7-Mediated Oncogenesis in Glandular Epithelium

edges.description: Gastric-type adenocarcinoma, the dominant HPV-independent subtype, characteristically presents with profuse watery vaginal discharge.
HPV-independent cervical adenocarcinoma also presents with abnormal vaginal bleeding, though more often at advanced stage than HPV-associated tumors.
Silva Pattern C adenocarcinomas carry approximately 22.5% risk of regional lymph node metastasis, justifying lymphadenectomy and adjuvant therapy.

nodes.description: Persistent infection of endocervical glandular epithelial cells by high-risk HPV (with HPV-16, HPV-18, and HPV-45 dominant) leads to integration of viral DNA and dysregulated expression of E6 and E7. E6 promotes p53 degradation; E7 inactivates pRB. The resulting loss of cell-cycle and apoptotic control, combined with HPV-driven genomic instability, drives progression from adenocarcinoma in situ to invasive HPV-associated adenocarcinoma.
nodes.id: HPV E6/E7-Mediated Oncogenesis in Glandular Epithelium

evidence.reference_title: Cervical adenocarcinoma: integration of HPV status, pattern of invasion, morphology and molecular markers into classification
evidence.snippet: It has become evident that human papillomavirus (HPV) infection does not drive all adenocarcinomas, and appropriate classification is critical for patient management, especially in the era of the HPV vaccine and HPV‐only screening.

nodes.description: A subset of cervical adenocarcinomas arises without HPV involvement, driven instead by recurrent somatic alterations in TP53, KRAS, CDKN2A, STK11, and other oncogenes/tumor suppressors. MSK-IMPACT prospective sequencing of 177 cervical cancers found PIK3CA (25%), ERBB2 (12%), KMT2C (10%), and KMT2D (9%) as the most prevalent alterations across cervical cancer histologies; STK11 alterations were enriched in gastric/clear-cell-associated tumors (33%, 7/21).

evidence.reference_title: Assessing the Genomic Landscape of Cervical Cancers: Clinical Opportunities and Therapeutic Targets
evidence.snippet: The most prevalent genomic alterations were somatic mutations or amplifications in PIK3CA (25%), ERBB2 (12%), KMT2C (10%), and KMT2D (9%).

evidence.reference_title: Research progress on human papillomavirus-negative cervical cancer: A review
evidence.snippet: HPV-negative cervical cancer has a different pathogenesis from HPV-positive cervical cancer

nodes.description: The Silva pattern classification (Pattern A, B, C) stratifies HPV-associated endocervical adenocarcinomas by the architecture of stromal invasion and directly predicts the risk of lymph node metastasis. Pattern A tumors are well-demarcated with no lymphovascular invasion or nodal disease; Pattern B shows focal destructive invasion; Pattern C is diffusely destructive and is associated with approximately 22.5% nodal metastasis, driving more aggressive surgical and adjuvant management.

evidence.reference_title: Cervical adenocarcinoma: integration of HPV status, pattern of invasion, morphology and molecular markers into classification
evidence.snippet: how HPV status, pattern of invasion as described by Silva and colleagues, histological features and molecular markers can be used to refine diagnosis and prognostication for patients with cervical adenocarcinoma
